skill.name: deployment-build
skill.description: Documents a Vercel build flow for a Next.js app with Docusaurus docs and a FastAPI backend.
skill.summary: Explains the project build sequence, Vercel routing, and required deployment variables.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell execution and environment access patterns, but the file is Markdown documentation rather than executable code. The build commands and secret variable names are legitimate deployment guidance, with low residual risk if an agent runs commands without user confirmation.

low_findings.title: Markdown Build Commands May Be Executed by Agents
low_findings.description: The listed npm, npx, and Next.js commands are deployment documentation, not executable skill code. They can still run package scripts if an assistant executes them during deployment work.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command strings are clearly present in Markdown and are operationally meaningful. Confidence is high that they are not hidden execution, but they remain relevant command guidance.

low_findings.title: Secret Variable Names Are Documented Without Values
low_findings.description: The skill names deployment secrets such as GEMINI_API_KEY, QDRANT_API_KEY, and BETTER_AUTH_SECRET. No secret values, environment reads, or network exfiltration behavior were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file only lists variable names for Vercel configuration. There is no code path that reads or transmits environment values.

low_findings.title: Cryptography and Reconnaissance Matches Lack Supporting Context
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance findings appear to match generic wording in the description and headings. No cryptographic operation, host probing, or system enumeration instruction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain front matter and a section heading only. The surrounding context does not support a security threat.

dangerous_patterns.title: Inline Shell Commands in Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes build command examples for npm, npx, and Next.js. These are not executed by the skill, but assistants should ask before running them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and grouped under build process documentation. The file format confirms they are Markdown examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Deployment Secret Names
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references API key and auth secret variable names. It does not include secret values or instructions to reveal them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The variable names are visible in a deployment checklist. No code reads those variables or sends them elsewhere.

content.user_title: Plan Vercel Builds for Hybrid Apps
content.value_statement: Hybrid web projects often fail when docs, app, and API builds are deployed separately. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise deployment context for the project build sequence and Vercel settings.
actual_capabilities: Explains the combined Next.js and Docusaurus build order.
Identifies the documented Vercel configuration file path.
Describes routing of API requests to a Python FastAPI backend.
Lists required Vercel environment variable names.
Helps assistants reason about deployment failures in this project.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automation.
Does not validate that Vercel settings are correct in a live project.
Does not provide secret values or manage environment variables.
Covers only the deployment details documented in SKILL.md.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Vercel deployment checklist
use_cases.description: Turn the documented build order and environment variables into a review checklist before release.

use_cases.title: Debug a failed hybrid build
use_cases.description: Use the build sequence to identify whether docs, static copy, or the Next.js build failed.

use_cases.title: Explain project deployment setup
use_cases.description: Summarize how the frontend, documentation site, and API route handling fit together.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Deployment Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain this project's Vercel deployment flow in simple steps. Include the docs build, static copy step, main app build, and API routing.

prompt_templates.title: Create Pre-Deploy Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise pre-deploy checklist for this project using the documented build commands and required Vercel environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Build Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: A Vercel build failed. Walk through likely failure points in the documented hybrid build process and suggest checks for each step.

prompt_templates.title: Review Configuration Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the deployment setup for operational risks. Focus on build command order, copied documentation assets, API route rewrites, and required secret variables.

output_examples.input: Explain what runs during the build.
output_examples.output: The documentation app is installed and built first. Its static output is copied into the Next.js public docs folder. The main Next.js app then builds for deployment.

output_examples.input: What should I check before deploying?
output: Confirm the docs app builds successfully.
Confirm the public docs output folder can be created.
Confirm API routes rewrite to the Python backend.
Confirm all required Vercel variables are configured.

best_practices: Confirm with the user before running build commands.
Store real secret values only in the deployment platform.
Check docs output before starting the final Next.js build.
anti_patterns: Do not paste secret values into chat or source files.
Do not skip the Docusaurus build when docs must be deployed.
Do not assume Vercel routes work without checking the backend configuration.

faq.question: Does this skill run deployment commands?
faq.answer: No. It documents the build process and configuration details for an assistant to reference.

faq.question: Which platforms does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Vercel deployment for a Next.js app, Docusaurus docs, and a FastAPI backend.

faq.question: Does it include secret values?
faq.answer: No. It lists variable names only. Secret values must remain in the Vercel dashboard.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it fix a failed build automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can guide diagnosis, but changes and command execution require user approval.

faq.question: What is the main deployment risk?
faq.answer: The main risk is running project build commands without reviewing package scripts and environment setup first.
